title: Phenol/chloroform genomic DNA extraction-1.5 ml Eppendorf tube.
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9tea2","text":"You will need to make the 20 mL of Lysis Buffer prior to running this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"39kg6","text":"Do all Phenol and Chloroform procedures in a fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a centrifuge, spin cells down in a 50 mL conical tube at maximum speed for 20 minutes at 4º C. 00:20:00, Maximum speed Maximum speeds vary, adjust according to individual availability.

Per cell pellet, add lysis buffer in multiples of 700 μL per pellet. undetermined For example, 1.4 mL, 2.1 mL, etc., depending on pellet volume. Lysis Buffer for Phenol/ chloroform genomic DNA extraction (makes 20 mL of buffer) by Daniel J. Richter
Pass sample through a 1 mL or a 5 mL syringe fitted with a 21 gauge needle, twenty times.
Spin cells down again for 20 minutes at maximum speed at 4º C, to pellet unlysed cellular material. 00:20:00 Maximum speeds vary, adjust according to individual availability.
Split the supernatant from each conical tube into 700 μL aliquots and place into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. If desired, retain mostly bacterial unlysed pellets for later use.
Add 3.5 μL RNAse A per tube (4 mg/ml, final concentration 20 ng/μl). undetermined, RNAse A
Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature on a nutator. 00:05:00
Incubate at 50º C for 3 hours, swirling occasionally. 03:00:00
Add 7 μL of Proteinase K (at 10 mg/mL, final concentration of 100 ng/μL). undetermined, Proteinase K
Cool to room temperature.
Add 700 μL of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol at a pH of 8.0, per tube. undetermined, per tube All phenol:chloroform steps should be performed in the chemical hood.
In hood, shake vigorously to mix.
In centrifuge, spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed, at room temperature. 00:10:00
Transfer aqueous phase (upper) into a new tube.
Repeat phenol:chloroform:isoamyl extraction (steps 11-14 above) until no protein remains at interphase. This can take 3-4 times.
Add an equal volume of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (no phenol), and shake vigorously to mix.
Spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed, at room temperature. 00:10:00
Remove aqueous phase (upper), combining into a single tube with room for 3X the volume of sample. Can use a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube or a 15 mL conical.
Add 1/20 of the volume of 10 M ammonium acetate, for a final concentration of 0.5 M.
Add 1/3 μL of GlycoBlue per 100 μL of aqueous phase.(at 15 mg/mL, final concentration 50 ng/μL)
Add 2x the volume of 100% EtOH.
Invert 5-10 times to precipitate DNA.
Precipitate several hours, or overnight at -20º C.
Spin at maximum speed at room temperature for 20 minutes. 00:20:00
Pour off supernatant.
Add 1 mL or 10 mL of 100% EtOH to wash pellet. Volume of EtOH to use depends on whether the pellet is in a 1.5 mL or 15 mL tube.
Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00
Spin 5-20 mintues at maximum speed. 00:20:00
Repeat 100% EtOH wash, for a total of 2-3 washes.
Pour off supernatant.
Add 1 mL or 10 mL of 70% EtOH, depending on tube size, to wash pellet.
Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00
Spin 5-20 minutes at maximum speed. 00:20:00
Remove supernatant, dry pellet for 5-10 minutes, do not over dry. 00:10:00
Re-suspend overnight in a small volume (~50-500 μL) of DNAse-free water.
Pour off supernatant.
Repeat 70% EtOH wash for a total of 2-3 washes. Volume of EtOH to use depends on whether the pellet is in a 1.5 mL or 15 mL tube.
creator: Daniel J. Richter
